expectations.text: Response adds KV namespace binding to wrangler.toml
expectations.evidence:
Response includes wrangler.toml config: [[kv_namespaces]]
binding = "PRODUCT_CACHE"
id = "<namespace-id-from-step-1>"

expectations.text: Response updates Env interface with PRODUCT_CACHE: KVNamespace
expectations.evidence: Code includes: export interface Env { PRODUCT_CACHE: KVNamespace; }

expectations.text: Response implements cache-first pattern (check KV, fallback to fetch)
expectations.evidence: Handler first checks KV with env.PRODUCT_CACHE.get(CATALOG_CACHE_KEY, 'json'), returns cached response with X-Cache: HIT header if found, otherwise fetches from origin API and stores result in KV
Code defines CATALOG_TTL = 86400 and uses it in env.PRODUCT_CACHE.put(CATALOG_CACHE_KEY, JSON.stringify(catalogData), { expirationTtl: CATALOG_TTL, ... })

expectations.text: Response provides CLI command to create KV namespace
expectations.evidence: Step 1 includes: bunx wrangler kv namespace create PRODUCT_CACHE
